KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in the truth, and not only I but also all who know the truth,
text.Rotherham: The Elder, unto an elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth; and not, I, alone, but all those also who understand the truth,—
text.KJV: The Elder vnto the elect Lady, and her children, whome I loue in the trueth: and not I onely, but also all they that haue knowen þ trueth:
text.NABRE: The Presbyter to the chosen Lady and to her children whom I love in truth—and not only I but also all who know the truth—
text.DRB: The Senior to the Lady Elect and her children, whom I love in truth, and not I only, but also all that have known the truth,

text.NRSV: because of the truth that abides in us and will be with us forever:
text.Rotherham: For the sake of the truth that abideth in us, and, with us, shall be unto times age-abiding,
text.KJV: For the trueths sake which dwelleth in vs, and shalbe with vs for euer:
text.NABRE: because of the truth that dwells in us and will be with us forever.
text.DRB: for the truth which abideth in us, and shall be with us for ever.

text.NRSV: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Father’s Son, in truth and love.
text.Rotherham: Favour, mercy, peace shall be with us, from God the Father, and from Jesus Christ the Son of the Father, in truth and love.
text.KJV: Grace bee with you, mercie, and peace from God the Father, and from the Lord Iesus Christ, the Sonne of the Father in trueth and loue.
text.NABRE: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ the Father’s Son in truth and love.
text.DRB: Grace be with you, mercy, peace from God the Father, and from Christ Jesus the Son of the Father in truth, and charity.

text.NRSV: I was overjoyed to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as we have been commanded by the Father.
text.Rotherham: I rejoiced exceedingly, in that I had found, from among thy children, such as were walking in truth, even as, a commandment, we received from the Father.
text.KJV: I reioyced greatly, that I found of thy children walking in trueth, as wee haue receiued a commaundement from the Father.
text.NABRE: I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in the truth just as we were commanded by the Father.
text.DRB: I was exceeding glad, because I have found of thy children walking in truth, as we have received commandment of the Father.

text.NRSV: But now, dear lady, I ask you, not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but one we have had from the beginning, let us love one another.
text.Rotherham: And, now, I request thee, lady, not as writing, a new commandment, unto thee, but one which we were holding from the beginning—That we should be loving one another.
text.KJV: And now, I beseech thee Lady, not as though I wrote a new commandement vnto thee: but that which wee had from the beginning, that wee loue one another.
text.NABRE: But now, Lady, I ask you, not as though I were writing a new commandment but the one we have had from the beginning: let us love one another.
text.DRB: And now I beseech thee Lady, not as writing a new commandment to thee, but that which we have had from the beginning, that we love one another.

text.NRSV: And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments; this is the commandment just as you have heard it from the beginning—you must walk in it.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is love, that we should be walking according to his commandments: This, is, the commandment, even as ye heard from the beginning, that, therein, ye should be walking.
text.KJV: And this is loue, that wee walke after his Commandements. This is the Commandement, that as yee haue heard from the beginning, yee should walke in it.
text.NABRE: For this is love, that we walk according to his commandments; this is the commandment, as you heard from the beginning, in which you should walk.
text.DRB: And this is charity, that we walk according to his commandments. For this is the commandment, that as you have heard from the beginning, you walk in the same.

text.NRSV: Many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh; any such person is the deceiver and the antichrist!
text.Rotherham: Because, many deceivers, have gone out into the world, they who do not confess Jesus Christ coming in flesh: This, is the deceiver and the antichrist.
text.KJV: For many deceiuers are entred into the world, who confesse not that Iesus Christ is come in the flesh. This is a deceiuer, and an Antichrist.
text.NABRE: Many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not acknowledge Jesus Christ as coming in the flesh; such is the deceitful one and the antichrist.
text.DRB: because many seducers are gone out into the world, which do not confess Jesus Christ to have come into flesh: this is a seducer and an Antichrist.

text.NRSV: Be on your guard, so that you do not lose what we have worked for, but may receive a full reward.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed unto yourselves, lest ye lose what things we earned,—but, a full reward, ye may duly receive.
text.KJV: Looke to your selues, that wee lose not those things which wee haue wrought, but that we receiue a full reward.
text.NABRE: Look to yourselves that you do not lose what we worked for but may receive a full recompense.
text.DRB: Look to yourselves, that you lose not the things which you have wrought: but that you may receive a full reward.

text.NRSV: Everyone who does not abide in the teaching of Christ, but goes beyond it, does not have God; whoever abides in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.
text.Rotherham: Every one that taketh a lead, and abideth not in the teaching of Christ, hath not, God: he that abideth in the teaching, the same, hath, both the Father and the Son.
text.KJV: Whosoeuer transgresseth and abideth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God: hee that abideth in the doctrine of Christ, he hath both the Father and the Sonne.
text.NABRE: Anyone who is so “progressive” as not to remain in the teaching of the Christ does not have God; whoever remains in the teaching has the Father and the Son.
text.DRB: Every one that revolteth, and persisteth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God. He that persisteth in the doctrine, the same hath both the Father, and the Son.

text.NRSV: Do not receive into the house or welcome anyone who comes to you and does not bring this teaching;
text.Rotherham: If anyone cometh unto you and, this teaching, doth not bring, be not receiving him home, and, Joy to thee! do not say;
text.KJV: If there come any vnto you, and bring not this doctrine, receiue him not into your house, neither bid him, God speed.
text.NABRE: If anyone comes to you and does not bring this doctrine, do not receive him in your house or even greet him;
text.DRB: If any man come to you, and bring not this doctrine, receive him not into the house, nor say, God save you, unto him.

text.NRSV: for to welcome is to participate in the evil deeds of such a person.
text.Rotherham: For, he that biddeth him rejoice, hath fellowship with his wicked works.
text.KJV: For hee that biddeth him God speed, is partaker of his euill deeds.
text.NABRE: for whoever greets him shares in his evil works.
text.DRB: For he that saith unto him, God save you, communicateth with his wicked works.

text.NRSV: Although I have much to write to you, I would rather not use paper and ink; instead I hope to come to you and talk with you face to face, so that our joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: Though I had many things, unto you, to write, I was not minded [to say them] with paper and ink; but I hope to come unto you, and, mouth to mouth, to talk,—that your joy may be made full.
text.KJV: Hauing many things to write vnto you, I would not write with paper and inke, but I trust to come vnto you, and speake face to face, that our ioy may be full.
text.NABRE: Although I have much to write to you, I do not intend to use paper and ink. Instead, I hope to visit you and to speak face to face so that our joy may be complete.
text.DRB: Having more things to write unto you: I would not by paper and ink: for I hope that I shall be with you, and speak mouth to mouth: that your joy may be full.

text.NRSV: The children of your elect sister send you their greetings.
text.Rotherham: The children of thy chosen sister salute thee.
text.KJV: The children of thy elect sister greet thee. Amen.
text.NABRE: The children of your chosen sister send you greetings.
text.DRB: The children of thy sister Elect salute thee.
